### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and contract workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.</br>\nDo not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

Report any work-related injury or illness for which the affected worker was treated by a doctor or licensed healthcare provider (e.g., stitches, prescription medication, minor surgery), and returned to either regular or light duties on the next scheduled day of work.\n
Cabinet Resolution No. 33 of 2022, Article 3 and Article 6

Please enter the number of work-related injuries or illnesses that required medical treatment by a licensed healthcare professional (beyond simple first aid) but did not result in any full days lost from work (excluding the day of the incident) and did not cause permanent disability.

Include only cases managed on site by designated first aiders (e.g., small cuts, minor burns), where the employee returns immediately to normal duties. These must be recorded internally.\n
Dubai First Aid Manual (Admin. Res. 7/2022)\n
Cabinet Resolution No. 33 of 2022
Please enter the number of injuries or illnesses requiring only simple first aid, with no lost time, work restriction, or further professional medical care.

These are unplanned events—such as serious equipment failures, hazardous material releases, fires, explosions, or other situations—which did not result in injury, death, or occupational disease but had the potential to cause significant harm.
